name: Desembocadura del río Sénia – circular desde Alcanar por la Ermita del Remei
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alcanar offers diverse terrain, from the rugged slopes of the Sierra del Montsià to the flat, expansive landscapes of the Ebro Delta. The region features a mix of challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes and gentler coastal paths. Riders can explore areas with panoramic views of the Alcanar plain and Alfacs Bay, as well as routes through natural environments. This variety provides options for different skill levels and preferences. Best mountain bike trails in Alcanar The most popular mountain bike trail is Desembocadura del río Sénia – circular desde Alcanar por la Ermita del Remei, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete, offering coastal views and a historic hermitage. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Wheelbarrow – View of the Cement Factory loop from Alcanar, a moderate 24.5 miles (39.4 km) path. This route features varied terrain with open views. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Cement Factory – The Wheelbarrow loop from la Ràpita, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through coastal and inland areas, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcanar is defined by the rugged Sierra del Montsià, the flat Ebro Delta, and scenic coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from technical mountain ascents to relaxed rides. The routes in Alcanar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 37 reviews. More than 268 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcanar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alcanar?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Alcanar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 268 mountain bikers using komoot to discover the area's varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alcanar?
A: Alcanar offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find rugged, technical trails with significant elevation changes in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Montsià&lt;/strong&gt;, ideal for more challenging rides. In contrast, the nearby &lt;strong&gt;Ebro Delta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides flat, expansive landscapes perfect for less strenuous, scenic rides focused on nature observation. Coastal paths and the Alcanar plain offer a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces with panoramic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Alcanar has 12 easy mountain bike routes. The flat terrain of the Ebro Delta is particularly well-suited for less strenuous rides. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38245930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Sénia River – Plaza de Toros de Vinaròs loop from Carrer de Monturiol, 2&lt;/a&gt;, which features minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find more challenging mountain bike routes in Alcanar?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Montsià&lt;/strong&gt; offers rugged and technical trails with significant elevation changes. There are 4 difficult routes available, providing a thrilling experience for those looking for advanced mountain biking.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Alcanar?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Alcanar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950638147/desembocadura-del-rio-senia-circular-desde-alcanar-por-la-ermita-del-remei" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desembocadura del río Sénia – circular desde Alcanar por la Ermita del Remei&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal views and passes by a historic hermitage.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails around Alcanar offer access to various natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique wetlands of the Ebro Delta, enjoy panoramic views of the Alcanar plain and Alfacs Bay, and discover points of interest like the historic &lt;strong&gt;Ermita del Remei&lt;/strong&gt;. Other notable natural highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch — View of the Ebro Delta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Alcanar?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in scenic viewpoints. Many routes offer stunning panoramic views of the Alcanar plain, the Alfacs Bay, and the Ebro Delta. Specific viewpoints you might encounter include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3297406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bayshore Ecological Reserve Observation Deck&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3759130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encanyissada Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing excellent spots to pause and take in the scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alcanar?
A: Alcanar, being part of the Costa Daurada, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and less intense heat than summer. These seasons allow for comfortable rides through both mountain and coastal areas, making it easier to enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Alcanar?
A: Yes, the flatter sections, particularly within the Ebro Delta Natural Park, are well-suited for family rides. These areas provide unique, magical landscapes with rich biodiversity, making for an enjoyable and less strenuous experience for all ages. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' for the most family-friendly options.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Alcanar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alcanar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 37 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from the challenging climbs of the Sierra del Montsià to the serene, flat paths of the Ebro Delta, as well as the stunning coastal and panoramic views encountered along the routes.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads for mountain bike routes?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many mountain bike routes in Alcanar have accessible starting points. It's common to find parking options near popular trailheads or within the towns and villages that serve as access points to the trails. For specific routes, checking the tour details on komoot can often provide information about starting locations.

Q: Are there any regulations or permits required for mountain biking in Alcanar?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails in Alcanar and the surrounding natural areas does not require specific permits for individual riders. However, it's always advisable to respect local signage, stay on marked trails, and be aware of any specific regulations that might apply to protected areas like the Ebro Delta Natural Park to ensure responsible riding.
tips: The Remei hermitage is located in a sensational natural and landscape environment, with beautiful panoramic views of the Alcanar plain, the Alfacs bay and the Ebro Delta. The complex, made up of a hermitage and a guesthouse, was built in two different times. The oldest nave is in the Gothic style, while the extension to the building and its characteristic dome are in the Baroque style.
Excellent panoramic views of the coastline and, in the distance, the Alfacs Bay and part of the Ebro Delta.

Bunkers located between Playa del Marjal and Estanyet and Camaril beaches, in Casas de Alcanar. These machine gun nests from the Spanish Civil War of 1936 were made up of trenches, shelters and powder kegs.

Nice place and very quiet. The Sénia River is the natural border that divides the Valencian Community from that of Catalonia. This particular point is the mouth of the river in the sea, so you can choose to take a bath in fresh or salt water ;)

waypoints.name: View of the Ebro Delta and the Bay of Alfacs
